label: Human evaluation & preference studies

node.label: Human evaluation & preference studies
node.definition: Human evaluation measures quality, preference, usefulness, risk, or experience through controlled studies, rubrics, pairwise comparisons, and expert review.
node.why_it_matters: Human evaluation supplies direct judgments of quality, preference, usability, and risk when automatic metrics are incomplete or misaligned.
limitations: Human ratings can be noisy, biased, expensive, and sensitive to rubric design, sampling, annotator expertise, and aggregation choices.

dependencies.rationale: Human evaluation uses statistics and experimental design to sample tasks, compare systems, and aggregate judgments.
Preference studies produce comparisons and rankings that can feed preference-learning workflows.
Human evaluation often records labels, targets, ratings, rewards, or preferences as supervision signals.
